title: brassica rupestris subsp. villosa
intro: Brassica rupestris subsp. villosa, also known as Alpine Cabbage or Wild Cabbage, is a subspecies of the Brassica rupestris plant, belonging to the Brassicaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which make it useful in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Alpine Cabbage can be prepared in various ways, including as a tea, infusion, or raw in salads, allowing for different levels of extraction and bioavailability of its active compounds.
